Because marketing rarely can finish the job. It can set up the job. So CNBC reported yesterday that 94% of all commerce in the United States in 2013 occurred offline. Yeah. So 6% is a pretty small sandbox. And then if you wanted to take out pure entertainment from Netflix to porn and then you would pretty much have Amazon and maybe a point or two for everybody else. Right. And and even that's deceptive because some of what gets counted as e-commerce actually had a salesperson involved at some place at that. You know not to be fair that media drives a lot of offline commerce as well. But regardless of how you want to fool with that number the overwhelming majority of sales requires sales. It can't be done just with marketing and media. And it is often the place where everything we do at GKIC for GKIC members sort of comes to a screeching halt and collapses into dysfunction. So I have two clients right now who by their own admission now have infinitely better marketing driving infinitely better and more leads into a horribly dysfunctional. End of game sales place with horribly dysfunctional inept sales people. And so all we've really accomplished at this point is exposed how bad that problem is. And often and how it must be fixed. And oftentimes marketing gets blamed as well. Right. Well I didn't we didn't make any money from this marketing that we set up. Advertising and marketing almost always bears the blame because the fix for that gets delegated. The fix for the sales function often has to be done by the owner who often is part of the problem even to the point that the owner could be one of the salespeople and be inept dysfunction etc. So the first place all the blame goes is advertising. Right. Is which generally that that discussion is that advertising brings us bad prospects. That advertising brings us badly. Then they'll blame the marketing. Right. Which that is that whole process really doesn't work. Right. Because it had at the end we're not selling what we thought we would be selling. But often the advertising is fine. The marketing is fine. I mean I had a client some years ago who spent two million dollars with me and I begged them to take a million of it off the table and use it to fix the sales component part of their business. I said just pay me less. Well let me do less. But pay me less and take this money and here's what you need to do with it. You need to fix the inbound call. You need to fix the sales person who is actually face to face with this patient's life's client. Please please please please please please. No we want better ads.
